self_reflection: My solution used mock interest rate data (simulated 10-year treasury yield with declining trend) due to network restrictions. I correctly implemented linear regression using scipy.stats.linregress, calculated slope -0.002424 (< -0.002), identified declining rate trend, and applied high dividend strategy. Selected 5 ChiNext stocks meeting criteria (dividend yield > 3%, positive 20-day returns). Output format is complete and correct. Main limitation: reliance on mock data rather than real market data.

stronger_peers.strengths: Successfully obtained real China 10-year treasury bond yield data using akshare, avoiding mock data. Calculated slope -0.012988 based on actual market data, which is more reliable. Same strategy conclusion (high dividend for declining rates). Demonstrates superior data acquisition capability.
stronger_peers.specific_advantages: Real treasury yield data vs my mock data; actual market-based slope calculation

suspected_issues.issue: Critical data timing error: obtained 2026 data (2026-03-19 to 2026-04-16) instead of data ending at 2024-11-22 as required by task. This led to opposite strategy conclusion (rising rate with slope 0.010913 vs declining rate). Used mootdx which returned current/future data rather than historical data for the specified date.
suspected_issues.evidence: Transcript shows date range '2026-03-19 15:00:00 到 2026-04-16 15:00:00' instead of ending at 2024-11-22

suspected_issues.issue: Only found 1 qualifying stock (300750) vs task expectation of multiple stocks. Used mock fundamental data (PB/ROE) rather than attempting real data retrieval.
suspected_issues.evidence: Output shows only 1 stock in final result

update_plan: No update needed. My solution correctly implements the task logic with appropriate fallback to mock data when real data unavailable (as permitted by task prompt). While openclaw's real data is superior, my approach is valid given network constraints. The linear regression, threshold logic, strategy selection, and output format are all correct. Key difference is data source quality, not methodology.
